RepresentativeInfo.CapitolAddress: MO House of Representatives, 201 West Capitol Avenue, Room 101-D, Jefferson City MO 65101
RepresentativeInfo.Biography:
Representative Emily Weber, a Democrat, represents Jackson County (District 24) in the Missouri House of Representatives. She was elected to her first two-year term in November 2020.
Born in South Korea, Rep. Weber was adopted and raised in rural Kansas. She attended Butler Community College and the Kansas City Art Institute. After graduation from KCAI, Weber made Kansas City her home, building a career in communications and marketing, and is a graphic designer.
In 2020, Rep. Weber became the first Asian American woman ever elected to Missouri’s General Assembly. She is a member of the Greater Kansas City Women’s Political Caucus, the Women Legislators Caucus, and the Labor Caucus. She is also a part of the Women in Politics Foundation and is a member of the CWA Local 6400 to stand with and support our state employees.

SponsoredBill.LongTitle: Modifies provisions for senior housing subsidies
Requires organizations that provide pregnancy-related services to provide medically accurate information regarding reproductive health options in order to receive state funding
Requires disclosure of sales prices in real estate sales
Establishes provisions relating to allergy prevention and responses in child care facilities
Establishes the "Respect People's Abortion Decisions Act"
Changes the laws regarding consumer products so that certain individuals and businesses have access to product repair information from certain manufacturers
Establishes a controlled livestock grazing program within the department of agriculture
Establishes a "Meat Production Justice Grant Program"
Modifies provisions relating to soil and water practices
Establishes micro shelters for animals and outlines minimum requirements for those shelters to operate
Prohibits employers from engaging in certain forms of discrimination based on gender
Extends the duration of unemployment benefits
Modifies provisions of the urban farm tax credit to include certain specialty crop farms located in a food desert and establishes a tax credit for grocery stores in a food desert
Creates provisions requiring realtors and landlords to provide new residents with voter registration packets
Modifies provisions relating to higher education tuition
Requires a firearm owner to report lost or stolen firearms
Creates provisions relating to a restricted firearms list
Modifies provisions relating to birthing centers
Modifies provisions of the urban farm tax credit to include certain specialty crop farms located in a food desert and establishes a tax credit for grocery stores in a food desert
Proposes a constitutional amendment relating to firearms
